articles.title: New Boss for a Big Radio Show
articles.summary: Rebecca Keating is the new editor of the Radio 4 Today programme, a popular news show on the BBC. She has worked at the BBC for almost 20 years on other important news programs. Rebecca says she is excited to lead the show and thinks it is very important because it helps people understand what is true and holds leaders accountable. The Today programme has over 5 million listeners. Rebecca will work with a team to make the show even better. She is taking over from Owenna Griffiths, who was the editor for more than five years. This change is part of keeping the show fresh and relevant for listeners.

articles.title: How President Trump's Tariffs Changed World Trade
articles.summary: One year ago, President Donald Trump started a trade war by putting taxes called tariffs on goods from other countries like China. He wanted to help American factories and make money for the government. Now, tariffs in the US are much higher, averaging about 10% compared to 2.5% before. This has changed global trade in four main ways. First, China fought back with its own tariffs, causing trade between the US and China to drop a lot. US imports from China fell by 30%, and exports to China fell by over 25%. Second, Chinese goods now make up less than 10% of US imports, down from over 20% in 2016. Third, the US is buying more from Vietnam and Mexico as Chinese companies invest there. Fourth, experts say this shift is likely to last, meaning trade won't go back to how it was before. Overall, Trump's tariffs have made trade between the US and China much smaller and changed where the US gets its goods from.

articles.title: New Rules to Stop Tricky Subscriptions
articles.summary: The government is making new laws to help people cancel subscriptions and get refunds more easily. These laws will stop companies from trapping people in subscriptions that renew automatically without them knowing. People will be able to cancel with just one click instead of making many phone calls. They will also get a 14-day cooling off period after a free trial ends or when a yearly subscription renews, so they can get a refund if they forget to cancel. Companies must give clear information and reminders before subscriptions renew. This could save people money each year. A person named Kim shared her story about having trouble canceling an anti-virus subscription and not getting a refund easily. The new laws are expected to start in spring 2027.

articles.title: SpaceX Goes Public: A Big Stock Market Event
articles.summary: SpaceX, a company that makes rockets and satellites, is planning to become a public company. This means people can buy shares of SpaceX on the stock market. The company filed papers with the SEC to do this secretly. It might be worth over $1 trillion when it goes public in June. Elon Musk, who owns SpaceX, could become very rich from this. SpaceX wants to raise $50 billion by selling shares. Recently, SpaceX took over another company called xAI, which makes AI. This helps SpaceX save money and share resources. Experts say SpaceX needs a lot of cash to grow and pay for things like computers and energy. This move shows SpaceX is getting ready for big changes.

articles.title: Carol Kirkwood Says Goodbye to Her Weather Job
articles.summary: Carol Kirkwood has worked for the BBC for 28 years, waking up very early at 2:45am to tell people the weather on TV. She is now retiring, which means she is stopping her job. She says she will miss her alarm clock and her friends at work. Carol loved meeting nice people while reporting the weather from places like Wimbledon and the Chelsea Flower Show. She got special gifts like a gold poppy and a weather station named after her, which made her feel happy and appreciated. She is 63 years old and from Scotland. Carol is sad to leave but excited for what comes next.

articles.title: A Family Race to Remember a Sister
articles.summary: Margo and Mark did not like each other when they first met over 40 years ago. Margo thought Mark was too serious, and Mark thought Margo was too loud. But they both loved Julia, who was Margo's sister and Mark's wife. Julia got sick and passed away in 2022. Before she died, she wanted Margo and Mark to do something special together. So they joined a TV show called Race Across the World. In this race, teams travel without phones or planes and with little money to win a prize. Margo and Mark became the first in-laws to compete. They raced to honor Julia's last wish and to become closer as family. The race was hard, but it helped them remember Julia and work together.
